what_it_does: A static HTML/CSS desktop mockup of the A2 canvas operating surface: it renders a running chain with two content nodes (codebase, constant) feeding an ask process node that feeds an embed process node, alongside a left node palette, a right inspector panel, a top toolbar, a footer activity strip, and semantic-zoom hints.
observations: Uses design-system.css and a custom <style> block with CSS variables for theming.
Top bar contains brand identity, a presence dial, and verb buttons (layers, wire, force, run).
Left rail palette groups nodes into only two live kinds: Process (11 items) and Content (5 items).
Canvas uses inline SVG paths for wires, colored by the source node's kind.
Nodes are absolutely positioned and include status dots, typed port nubs, port labels, and config pills.
The selected ask process node shows a model config pill (deepseek-v4-pro) and a system prompt.
The embed process node outputs a 1024-dimensional Vector.
A semantic-zoom hint shows zoom=0.78 (expanded) and a collapsed zchip shows what nodes become below zoom 0.5.
Legend displays only the two real kinds and the four real statuses: idle, ran, cached, stuck.
Right panel is a tabbed inspector for the selected ask node with inputs and action buttons.
Footer reports live execution state: ran ask, ran embed, 1 cached constant, codebase volatile→ran.
A mobile tabbar is present but intended to be hidden on desktop via design-system.css.
Large HTML comment block maps every represented concept to backend code references and explicitly lists things that must not be drawn.
standouts: The file doubles as a normative design document, using comments to forbid fictional UI elements.
It insists model is a config pill, not a node kind, and that only process and content are live node kinds.
It restricts node status dots to idle/ran/cached/stuck and states await is inbox/presence only.
Typed ports are surfaced with small labels (Text, Vector, Any).
Semantic zoom is made concrete via a zoom hint and a collapsed chip example.
conventions: CSS custom properties from an external design-system.css.
data-ui-ref attributes marking UI elements for cross-reference.
Semantic HTML regions: header, nav, main, aside, footer.
Inline SVG for connection wires between nodes.
Comments used as structured design documentation.
concerns: Many CSS classes (.app, .btn, .node, .bar, .body, .cfg, .pill, .addr, .sdot, .port, .tabbar, etc.) are used but not defined in this file; they depend entirely on design-system.css.
The comment says ask→embed is the 'freshly-fired edge' but the SVG path is solid, not dashed.
Node positions and wire coordinates are hardcoded, making the mockup fragile.
The mobile tabbar is included but no file-local CSS hides it on desktop.
Inline styles are heavily mixed with classes.
notes: A high-fidelity, annotated desktop mockup that also acts as a specification for what the canvas must and must not display.
questions: Where are the CSS rules for .app, .btn, .node, .port, .pill, and .tabbar defined?
Is the semantic-zoom threshold of 0.5 implemented in App.tsx or elsewhere?
Are node positions and wire paths hand-coded or generated from a layout algorithm?
What does RHM stand for in the tabs and palette?
Is the presence dial's eight-mode posture control documented in another file?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is a desktop canvas mockup for a running chain; the file matches that, while also serving as a normative design document with backend cross-references in comments.
apparent_themes: Surface only real backend states and kinds in the UI.
Config slots (e.g., model) are not node kinds.
Explicit rejection of fictional UI elements.
intention_signals: To provide a single-source, annotated desktop canvas design that prevents drift between the visual mockup and the actual runtime model by explicitly forbidding fictional node kinds, statuses, and edges.
novelty: The extensive comment block that inventories real backend code references and declares what must not be drawn (no presentation node, no await/failed/running dot, no four-kind legend) is unusual for a static mockup.
contribution: A concrete, annotated desktop canvas layout and visual vocabulary (two node kinds, four status dots, typed ports, semantic zoom, model-as-pill) that a fused design system can reuse for the A2 canvas representation.
summary_for_embedding: Static HTML/CSS desktop mockup of the A2 operating canvas showing a running chain of content and process nodes with typed ports, semantic zoom hints, a node palette, inspector panel, and explicit annotations tying the visual model to backend code.
intention_for_embedding: To define the desktop A2 canvas view for a running chain and enforce fidelity to the real node kinds, statuses, and execution semantics.
